title: $1.3 Million Homes in Sweden
description: A 10-bedroom manor from 1806, a 25-acre farm estate near the Baltic Sea, and a five-bedroom modern farmhouse.
author: Roxana Popescu

title: A.I. Boom Leads to Record Home Prices in San Francisco
description: The Bay Area boasts the highest median home prices in the country and experienced the sharpest annual price increases.
author: Kami Rieck

title: How to Make Wallpaper Art
description: Start out with a low-stakes installation on an art canvas. You’ll learn the basics, and you can easily undo it.
author: Tim Heffernan and Clark Hodgin

title: Homes for Sale in New York and New Jersey
description: This week’s properties are a four-bedroom in Laurel, N.Y., and a five-bedroom in Bridgewater, N.J.
author: Claudia Gryvatz Copquin and Jill P. Capuzzo

title: Homes for Sale in Brooklyn and Manhattan
description: This week’s properties are in Williamsburg, on the Upper West Side and in Hudson Heights.
author: Heather Senison

title: Still Mailing Your Rent? You’re Hardly Alone.
description: Those who pay their rent online are now the majority — but just barely.
author: Joyce Cohen

title: A House That Adapts to Party Mode, Private Mode and Guest Mode
description: A London couple turned their four-bedroom house into a one-bedroom shape-shifter — and got a lot more room for living.
author: Tim McKeough

title: America’s Last Surviving Wallace Neff Bubble House Is for Sale
description: Located in Pasadena, Calif., this futuristic dome — and affordable housing prototype — still stuns.
author: Linda Laban

title: $2.8 Million Homes in New York, Pennsylvania and Tennessee
description: A 19th-century farmhouse in Red Hook, N.Y., a Frank Lloyd Wright-inspired cottage in Port Allegany, Pa., and a 1949 house in Franklin, Tenn.
author: Angela Serratore

title: Mamdani’s Housing Plan Aims to Shore Up NYCHA and Build Affordable Homes
description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani unveiled a sweeping housing plan that calls for building 200,000 affordable units and seeks to improve New York City’s crumbling public housing system.
author: Emma G. Fitzsimmons and Jeffery C. Mays

title: Sometimes, the Best Way to Explore a Landscape Is to Sit Down
description: Madoo, in Sagaponack, N.Y., is giving visitors a different way to view a garden, with outdoor seating in a variety of styles and colors.
author: Margaret Roach

title: My HOA Neighbor Is Breaking the Rules. What Can I Do?
description: There are contracts between property owners, and also between owners and the community.
author: Jill Terreri Ramos

title: Is Long Beach Island’s Audacious Architecture Being Washed Away?
description: The beloved summer getaway off the Jersey Shore has been a playground for modernism. But a new wave of Hamptons-style mansions may steal its identity.
author: Michael Harmon and Ashok Sinha

title: $1 Million Homes in Curaçao
description: Willemstad, the Caribbean island’s capital, offers Caribbean-style villas, modernist homes, and indoor-outdoor specialties.
author: Alison Gregor

title: New Jersey Says Yes to Airbnbs During the World Cup
description: As New York City tamps down on short-term rentals, some New Jersey municipalities are encouraging residents to take advantage of the windfall World Cup travelers could bring.
author: Addie Morfoot and Ahmed Gaber

title: Digging Deep Into Dog Poop Data: Which Neighborhood Is the Worst?
description: The city relies on 311 complaints to log dog waste. But one resident took a closer look.
author: Joyce Cohen

title: Homes for Sale in Brooklyn and Manhattan
description: This week’s properties are in Bushwick, Kips Bay and Midtown East.
author: Heather Senison

title: Homes for Sale in New York and Connecticut
description: This week’s properties are in Chappaqua, N.Y., and New Fairfield, Conn.
author: Anne Mancuso and Alicia Napierkowski

title: Return to the Land, a Whites-Only Community, Is Sued for Discrimination
description: Return to the Land, a 160-acre development requiring members be white and heterosexual, is breaking fair housing and civil rights laws, according to a lawsuit.
author: Debra Kamin
